channel.title: Vitamin D and Vitamin A on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Vitamin D and Vitamin A on VitaminDWiki

item.title: COVID appears to be treated by many antioxidants (Vitamins D, C, E, K, and Quercetin, Curcumin, etc)
item.description:
• An emerging natural antioxidant therapy for COVID-19 infection patients: Current and future directions
      • VitaminDWiki - Prevent a COVID death: 9 dollars of Vitamin D or 900,000 dollars of vaccine - Aug 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - COVID fought by the antioxidants: Vitamin D, Zinc, Vitamin C, and Selenium – Review Sept 2022
      • Antioxidants and Long Covid - Nov 2022 preprint

An emerging natural antioxidant therapy for COVID-19 infection patients: Current and future directions

FoodFrontiers. 2023;1-28. DOI: 10.1002/fft2.207

item.title: HIV patients with low Vitamin A were 3.6 X more likely to get Tuberculosis
item.description:
The Impact of Vitamin A Deficiency on Tuberculosis Progression

Clinical Infectious Diseases, Volume 75, Issue 12, 15 December 2022, Pages 2178–2185, https://doi.org/10.1093/cid/ciac326

Brendan K Podell, Omowunmi Aibana, Chuan-Chin Huang, James E DiLisio, Macallister C Harris, David F Ackart, Kody Armann, Alexander Grover, Patrice Severe, Marc Antoine Jean Juste

Background

Although previous studies have shown that vitamin A deficiency is associated with incident tuberculosis (TB) disease, the direction of the association has not been established. We investigated the impact of vitamin A deficiency on TB disease progression.

item.title: Table salt was fortified with Vitamin A, Iodine, etc in Indian Trial
item.description:
• A Study to Improve the Vitamin A and Iodine Status of Pregnant Women through a Multiple Micronutrient Fortified Salt
      • Assumed 10 grams of daily salt, ribbon-blended in many nutrients, only reported on Vitamin A and kiodine
      • 2500 IU V-A, 30 mg ron, 300 µg iodine, 1 mg B1, 1 mg B2, 1 mg B6, 4 µg B12, 5 mg niacin, and 200 µg folic acid.
      • Strangely, those getting salt with Vitamin A were MORE LIKELY to be Vitamin A deficient in Q1 and Q2

A Study to Improve the Vitamin A and Iodine Status of Pregnant Women through a Multiple Micronutrient Fortified Salt

J Nutr Metab . 2022 Nov 10;2022:5301499. doi: 10.1155/2022/5301499

item.title: Excellent reviews of supplements at ConsumerLab
item.description:
• 200+ supplement reviews at ConsumerLab include:
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D is the 3rd most important contributor to health
      • VitaminDWiki - How non-US residents can buy high-dose Vitamin D and other supplements

200+ supplement reviews at ConsumerLab include:

Vitamin D, Omega-3, Magnesium, Vitamin K, B Vitamins, Probiotics, Turmeric/Curcumin, Ashwagandha, Iron, Quercetin, Resveratrol, Selenium, Tuna, Zinc

I have been subscribing to their reviews for over a decade ($54/year)
Each review includes

  • 10-100 pages of science (with about 95% correct interpretation)

item.title: How vitamins A, B, C, D, E, F (Omega), K fight COVID
item.description:
• Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory roles of vitamins in COVID-19 therapy

Antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory roles of vitamins in COVID-19 therapy

European Journal of Medicinal Chemistry 232 (2022) 114175 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ejmech.2022.114175

Aurelia Magdalena Pisoschi a’ *, Aneta Pop a, Florin Iordache a, Loredana Stanca a,

Ovidiu Ionut Geicu a, Liviu Bilteanu a’b, Andreea Iren Serban a’c

  • a University of Agronomic Sciences and Veterinary Medicine of Bucharest, Faculty of Veterinary Medicine, Department Preclinical Sciences, 105 Splaiul Independentei, 050097, Bucharest, Romania

item.title: COVID-19 decreased risk if add Vitamin D, A, C, NAC, Se, Zn, or Omega-3
item.description:
Vitamins and other immune-supportive elements as cofactors for passing the COVID-19 pandemic

Basic Appl Sci (2021) 10:71 https://doi.org/10.1186/s43088-021-00163-2

Haitham Saeed * , Hasnaa Osama1, Mona A. Abdelrahman1, Yasmin M. Madney1, Hadeer S. Harb1,

Mohamed E. A. Abdelrahim1 and Fatma Ali

Beni-Suef University Journal of Basic and Applied Sciences

Background: Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) is a viral disease that causes a respiratory disorder, started in December of 2019 in China. Several vitamins and trace elements could help in enhancing host immunity producing antioxidant or anti-inflammatory action. This work aimed to identify the role of different nutrition, vitamins, and trace elements on the immunity status of the infected subject and the possibility of the beneficial role of these elements in the management of COVID-19.

item.title: COVID-19 patients had low levels of Zinc, Vitamin A, Vitamin D, and Vitamin C
Vitamin D, C, A, and E, as well as Iron, Se, and Zinc each augment vaccine response
item.description:
The Role of Micronutrient and Immunomodulation effect in the vaccine era of COVID-19

J Chin Med Assoc. 2021 Jul 16. doi: 10.1097/JCMA.0000000000000587

Yu-Ju Lai 1, Huan-Shuo Chang, Yi-Ping Yang, Tzu-Wei Lin, Wei-Yi Lai, Yi-Ying Lin, Cheng-Chang Chang

#### Some of the nutrients in this study also prevent and/or treat COVID-19

item.title: Deficiencies in nutrients needed for a healthy immune system (Vitamin D, etc.)
item.description:
Inadequacy of Immune Health Nutrients: Intakes in US Adults, the 2005-2016 NHANES

Nutrients 2020, 12, 1735; doi:10.3390/nu12061735

Carroll A. Reider *, Ray-Yuan Chung, Prasad P. Devarshi, Ryan W. Grant and Susan Hazels Mitmesser

Science & Technology, Pharmavite LLC, West Hills, CA 91304, USA; raychung@umich.edu (R.-Y.C.); pdevarshi@pharmavite.net (P.P.D.); rgrant@pharmavite.net (R.W.G); smitmesser@pharmavite.net (S.H.M.)

  • Correspondence: creider@pharmavite.net; Tel.: +818-426-6111

Received: 16 May 2020; Accepted: 9 June 2020; Published: 10 June 2020

This study ignores additional nutrients needed for a health immune system.

item.title: Vitamin A is needed in some 3rd world pregnancies (but not too much)
